name: google-java-format-for-vs-code
displayName: Google Java Format for VS Code
description: Visual Studio Code extension to format Java files using google-java-format program to comply with Google Java Style.

configuration.title: Google Java Format for VS Code

java.format.settings.google.executable.markdownDescription: *Override (not recommended).* Specifies a URL or local file path to a formatter executable. **When set, `version`, `mode`, and `style` are all ignored.** Use this only when you need a binary that differs from those auto-downloaded by this extension.

java.format.settings.google.style.markdownDescription:
Selects the Java formatter. Ignored when `executable` is set.
- `google` ‚Äî [Google Java Format](https://github.com/google/google-java-format): the official Google Java style formatter. Supports both `jar-file` and `native-binary` modes.
- `palantir` ‚Äî [Palantir Java Format](https://github.com/palantir/palantir-java-format): 120-char lines, more lambda-friendly. **Only `native-binary` mode is supported.** Downloaded from Maven Central.
markdownEnumDescriptions: [Palantir Java Format](https://github.com/palantir/palantir-java-format). 120-char lines, more lambda-friendly. **Only `native-binary` mode is supported.** Binary downloaded from Maven Central. Not available on Windows or macOS x86-64.

java.format.settings.google.version.markdownDescription:
*Recommended.* Ignored when `executable` is set. Specifies the formatter version to use.
- For `google` style: a [Google Java Format](https://github.com/google/google-java-format/releases) release tag such as `1.25.2`, or `latest`.
- For `palantir` style: a [Palantir Java Format](https://github.com/palantir/palantir-java-format) Maven Central version such as `2.89.0`, or `latest`.

Default: `latest`.

java.format.settings.google.mode.markdownDescription: Specifies the runtime artifact to use. Ignored when `executable` is set. **Note:** `jar-file` mode is only available for `google` style; selecting it with `palantir` style will produce an error.
markdownEnumDescriptions: Run the formatter as a JAR file using the local Java runtime. **Only available for `google` style.** Requires a compatible Java runtime on PATH (Java 21+ for google-java-format ‚â• 1.22.0; older versions work with their respective minimum supported Java version).

Run the platform-specific native binary (no JVM required). Supported for both `google` and `palantir` styles. Falls back to `jar-file` when no native binary is available for the current platform (google style only).

java.format.settings.google.extra.markdownDescription: Extra CLI arguments to pass to the formatter (e.g. `--aosp` for 4-space Android Open Source Project style). Applied for both `google` and `palantir` styles.
